retrieved_docs.title: Cogan Syndrome -- Differential Diagnosis
retrieved_docs.content: Congenital syphilis: The Hutchinson triad of congenital syphilis includes Hutchinson teeth (notched peg-shaped upper incisors), interstitial keratitis, and sensorineural hearing loss. [38] Serology for syphilis must be negative for making a diagnosis of Cogan syndrome.

retrieved_docs.title: Congenital and Maternal Syphilis -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: Hutchinson teeth (Hypoplastic notched or peg-shaped permanent teeth, usually of the upper central incisors)

retrieved_docs.title: Congenital and Maternal Syphilis -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: Protuberant mandible

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Shape and Size -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: Microdontia often produces teeth with a conical or peg-shaped appearance, most commonly associated with developmental or syndromic conditions. True generalized microdontia, affecting the entire dentition without an underlying syndrome, is rare and warrants further evaluation. Macrodontia presents as teeth that are larger than expected, with generalized cases often linked to syndromic or systemic conditions. A rare variant, polarization, refers to macrodontia affecting premolars, causing them to resemble molars. Only 36 cases have been documented in the literature. [21] Patients with generalized macrodontia should undergo evaluation for potential underlying systemic or syndromic conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: Incontinentia Pigmenti (Bloch-Sulzberger Syndrome) -- History and Physical
retrieved_docs.content: Teeth: The features include microdontia, anodontia, hypodontia, and peg-shaped teeth. Some significant dental abnormalities are microdontia, delayed eruption of permanent teeth, and cleft palate. [30]

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Shape and Size -- Differential Diagnosis -- Macrodontia
retrieved_docs.content: Gemination Fusion Facial hemihypertrophy

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Number -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: Supernumerary teeth, if malpositioned, can prevent the normal eruption of developing teeth, which would require removal, a potentially invasive procedure. The multiple supernumerary teeth are generally indicative of an underlying syndromic or developmental condition, which the appropriate clinician should evaluate.

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Number -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Multiple supernumerary teeth rarely appear in patients without an associated syndrome or developmental disorder. In contrast, a single supernumerary tooth represents a relatively common dental anomaly and typically does not require further diagnostic workup. Supernumerary teeth most frequently occur in the anterior maxilla, mandibular premolar region, or around the third molars. [20]

retrieved_docs.content: Pituitary dwarfism Hypopituitarism Growth hormone-related defects

retrieved_docs.content: The differential diagnosis for each anomalous condition is outlined below. Additional diagnostic tools, such as radiographic evaluation and genetic testing, may aid in distinguishing between these conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: Posterior Crossbite -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: The etiology of a cross-bite deformity includes: Hereditary influence Inadequate dental arch length Overretained deciduous teeth Supernumerary teeth Habits like digit sucking Skeletal-anteroposterior discrepancy of arches Cleft lip and palate

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Number -- Etiology
retrieved_docs.content: The cause of hyperdontia remains unclear, with evidence pointing to multifactorial influences. In cases where multiple unerupted supernumerary teeth are present, clinicians should consider syndromic conditions, eg, cleidocranial dysplasia, a rare congenital skeletal disorder inherited in an autosomal dominant pattern. [5] Other syndromes frequently associated with multiple supernumerary teeth include cleft lip and/or palate, trichorhinophalangeal syndrome, and Gardner syndrome. [6]

retrieved_docs.content: Other bone diseases such: Osteogenesis imperfecta, congenital syphilis

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Shape and Size -- Differential Diagnosis -- Fusion
retrieved_docs.content: Macrodontia Gemination Talon cusp

retrieved_docs.title: Developmental Disturbances of the Teeth, Anomalies of Structure -- Deterrence and Patient Education
retrieved_docs.content: As all of the above conditions are genetic or developmental in origin, there is no true way to deter or prevent these conditions from occurring. Patients that present with generalized developmental defects affecting the entire dentition should be evaluated for an underlying syndrome. Treatment of the above-listed conditions is likely symptomatically or esthetically driven. Patients presenting with these conditions should be encouraged to self-monitor for new or worsening symptoms that dental professionals will evaluate and treat.

retrieved_docs.content: التصلب العصيدي is a medical condition that is associated with ذَبْهَةُ برنزميتال. Clinically, التصلب العصيدي is linked to several findings, including النفخة الانقباضية, حفظ الصحة الروتيني, عرج, and لغط الشريان السباتي. In addition, التصلب العصيدي has a recognized subtype called التصلب العصيدي المعمم, reflecting a more generalized form of the disease. These associations are important for recognizing the clinical presentation of التصلب العصيدي and guiding appropriate evaluation and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 4 -- Community 2873
retrieved_docs.content: The condition referred to as اضطراب جنيني (fetal disorder) is classified as a type of مضاعفات الحمل (pregnancy complication). Recognizing that fetal disorders are encompassed within the broader category of pregnancy complications helps clinicians identify, monitor, and manage these issues during prenatal care, ensuring appropriate assessment and intervention for both maternal and fetal health.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 3 -- Community 1843
retrieved_docs.content: Short gestation; low birth weight; and fetal growth retardation are classified under the term تقييد النمو الجنيني, which denotes fetal growth restriction. This condition is understood to be part of a broader, unspecified disorder that originates in the perinatal period, referred to as حالة غير محددة نشأت في الفترة المحيطة بالولادة. Recognizing that تقييد النمو الجنيني falls within this larger perinatal context helps clinicians assess and manage infants presenting with these growth‑related complications.
